headline: Propane and Progress: 'King of the Hill' Blazes Back with Landmark Reboot
lede: Hank Hill's suburban satire returns to streaming with a generational update that promises to recapture the animated comedy's razor-sharp social commentary.
body:
Hulu is rekindling the propane flame of Mike Judge's beloved animated series *King of the Hill*, dropping a full Season 15 slate that signals a bold reimagining of the beloved Arlen, Texas universe.

The revival promises to explore how the iconic Hill family navigates contemporary social landscapes, with original voice talents Mike Judge and Kathy Najimy returning to anchor the narrative. Series creators are betting that the show's signature blend of dry humor and heartland authenticity will resonate with both nostalgic fans and a new generation of viewers.

While specific plot details remain under wraps, industry insiders suggest the new season will tackle modern suburban dynamics with the same nuanced approach that made the original series a cult classic. The comedic lens will likely examine technological shifts, changing family structures, and the evolving cultural fabric of Texas suburbia.

The 10-episode order represents a significant recommitment to a franchise that has maintained passionate audience loyalty despite being off the air for years. Streaming platforms continue to demonstrate robust appetite for reviving beloved animated properties with multigenerational appeal.
key_facts: Marks first new season in over a decade
